title: podman: Copy doesn't seem to work from cache
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run `podman build` on a `Dockerfile` that uses `FROM .. AS ...` and `COPY --from` syntax. Example: https://github.com/ford-prefect/laser-egg/blob/master/Dockerfile

2. Make some changes around the second container.

3. Run `podman build` again

**Describe the results you received:**

An error like the following is seen:

```
% docker build .           
Emulate Docker CLI using podman. Create /etc/containers/nodocker to quiet msg.
STEP 1: FROM haskell:8.6 AS builder
STEP 2: WORKDIR /opt/laser-egg
--> Using cache 9df34abc65ed6df0ea5a53c43a2b84b12e50d635c260ac38554782422eb60b83
STEP 3: FROM 9df34abc65ed6df0ea5a53c43a2b84b12e50d635c260ac38554782422eb60b83 AS builder
STEP 4: ADD . .
--> Using cache cfbc6d928043c053a9fb378a7f9de6a9e6a22501d8109c03f7d101a0e0c730d6
STEP 5: FROM cfbc6d928043c053a9fb378a7f9de6a9e6a22501d8109c03f7d101a0e0c730d6 AS builder
STEP 6: RUN stack --install-ghc install && strip /root/.local/bin/laser-egg
--> Using cache 1dbd7dd8bd67b2a4faa9eb5a53165fe53766ed73347041cb30e93e7a0d2d4929
STEP 7: COMMIT 
--> 96b5fb8f4a76bdb85d2d3c77c9ee15d905cb1bdf7d5a64989142752d44b5eeb0
--> c9b71cb635aa8aac7318b7e9e64685d6454f3de89cad79f99d1ac5f75810e00a
STEP 8: FROM debian:stretch
STEP 9: ENV LANG            C.UTF-8
--> Using cache 73c7282f117c4af0d3eb21eb995a0aea33136a2652e6d0651b9771a0c6b6e5f9
STEP 10: FROM 73c7282f117c4af0d3eb21eb995a0aea33136a2652e6d0651b9771a0c6b6e5f9
STEP 11: RUN apt-get update &&     apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends libgmp10
--> Using cache 769fe1804b6a0afcab3919d955be86926af1656604fe95fb93478835e9ef3d7c
STEP 12: FROM 769fe1804b6a0afcab3919d955be86926af1656604fe95fb93478835e9ef3d7c
STEP 13: COPY --from=builder /root/.local/bin/laser-egg /usr/bin/laser-egg
Error: error building at step {Env:[PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin LANG=C.UTF-8] Command:copy Args:[/root/.local/bin/laser-egg /usr/bin/laser-egg] Flags:[--from=builder] Attrs:map[] Message:COPY --from=builder /root/.local/bin/laser-egg /usr/bin/laser-egg Original:COPY --from=builder /root/.local/bin/laser-egg /usr/bin/laser-egg}: no files found matching "/home/arun/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/6c4c42ae1b2e264ad337bbf00a01cea677c572828ff6c2bd8dfa6e5c946b5ea7/merged/root/.local/bin/laser-egg": no such file or directory
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

The expectation is that the cached image from the end of the previous `FROM` section be used.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

The problem seems to be that the wrong image is substituted in the second `FROM ...` section.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.2.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.12
  podman version: 1.2.0
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.7.2
  Conmon:
    package: podman-1.2.0-2.git3bd528e.fc30.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.12.0-dev, commit: a13eadcaf0f07ba590c96d845a03ac3a0da7c56d'
  Distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "30"
  MemFree: 12337438720
  MemTotal: 33335652352
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-92.dev.gitc1b8c57.fc30.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc7+dev
      commit: d38f8a2d00ff444e52d16ab1ea5bbe5c1cb471e8
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 16738152448
  SwapTotal: 16739463168
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 8
  hostname: stillness
  kernel: 5.0.13-300.fc30.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  uptime: 20h 38m 12.79s (Approximately 0.83 days)
insecure registries:
  registries: []
registries:
  registries:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ConfigFile: /home/arun/.config/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 11
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
  GraphRoot: /home/arun/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 11
  RunRoot: /tmp/1000
  VolumePath: /home/arun/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
Hi,

Here is a minimal Dockerfile with instructions to reproduce the bug:
```
FROM fedora as builder

RUN mkdir -p /dir1/dir2 && touch /dir1/dir2/file1 /dir1/dir2/file2 /dir1/dir2/file3

FROM fedora

RUN mkdir /dir1
COPY --from=builder /dir1 /dir1/
```

`podman build --no-cache -t test .`
```
STEP 1: FROM fedora AS builder
STEP 2: RUN mkdir -p /dir1/dir2 && touch /dir1/dir2/file1 /dir1/dir2/file2 /dir1/dir2/file3
--> 11b715ed9fdac79ce12bc426181ad38536bd3acfb5b00c842d66d133a77987c2
STEP 3: FROM fedora
STEP 4: RUN mkdir /dir1
--> 936161aab1fd8c3eb6dbe66c21a271a8ba6c9d2990c87fad0dbf12b5a74c9c7f
STEP 5: COPY --from=builder /dir1 /dir1/
STEP 6: COMMIT test
--> ad3ebfbad62f7ee5f42012974d793821a30b50fc1e2d0e75875c9511347f6c8c
```

`podman run -ti test /bin/ls /dir1/dir2`
```
file1  file2  file3
```

`podman build -t test .`
```
STEP 1: FROM fedora AS builder
STEP 2: RUN mkdir -p /dir1/dir2 && touch /dir1/dir2/file1 /dir1/dir2/file2 /dir1/dir2/file3
--> Using cache d716810b7e7ddfa927610db4b00801d7952ed7c070090fe4251d9cf202bf8ac0
STEP 3: FROM fedora
STEP 4: RUN mkdir /dir1
--> Using cache 5255c4dd19660c1e6ba6c3b93ce35ac362ba097123ae02399d269a7ef64d2326
STEP 5: COPY --from=builder /dir1 /dir1/
Error: error building at STEP "COPY --from=builder /dir1 /dir1/": no files found matching "/var/home/yann/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/fb446028084dbab62b8f0b6eb4e5dafb7a737c77ea36a842cd27cf46074108b6/merged/dir1": no such file or directory
```
Thanks - that should help a lot!

I tries the above minimal Dockerfile, looks like we have fixed this problem. 
```
podman version 1.4.5-dev
BuildahVersion: 1.9.2
```
```
$ podman build --no-cache -t test -f Dockerfile.cache .
STEP 1: FROM fedora AS builder
STEP 2: RUN mkdir -p /dir1/dir2 && touch /dir1/dir2/file1 /dir1/dir2/file2 /dir1/dir2/file3
709d6353b83eab333f4460f276afb3c22c382cd59f2b71c64236b0bf812d33d6
STEP 3: FROM fedora
STEP 4: RUN mkdir /dir1
52404a945ec68daa758f5ad720a951321d5379c84b64b7dd0f03319eedb39c38
STEP 5: COPY --from=builder /dir1 /dir1/
STEP 6: COMMIT test
60c4aec0288dce05215b3c70c751efbce0583085a5375afa8711870e1fe86240


$ podman build -t test -f Dockerfile.cache .
STEP 1: FROM fedora AS builder
STEP 2: RUN mkdir -p /dir1/dir2 && touch /dir1/dir2/file1 /dir1/dir2/file2 /dir1/dir2/file3
--> Using cache 709d6353b83eab333f4460f276afb3c22c382cd59f2b71c64236b0bf812d33d6
STEP 3: FROM fedora
STEP 4: RUN mkdir /dir1
--> Using cache 52404a945ec68daa758f5ad720a951321d5379c84b64b7dd0f03319eedb39c38
STEP 5: COPY --from=builder /dir1 /dir1/
--> Using cache 60c4aec0288dce05215b3c70c751efbce0583085a5375afa8711870e1fe86240
STEP 6: COMMIT test
60c4aec0288dce05215b3c70c751efbce0583085a5375afa8711870e1fe86240
$ podman run -ti test /bin/ls /dir1/dir2
file1  file2  file3
```

Hey, I don't believe this is fully fixed. Please consider the following slightly adjusted Dockerfile (copying a specific file instead of dir):

```
FROM fedora as builder

RUN mkdir -p /dir1/dir2 && touch /dir1/dir2/file1 /dir1/dir2/file2 /dir1/dir2/file3

FROM fedora

RUN mkdir /dir1
COPY --from=builder /dir1/dir2/file1 /dir1/
```
```
╰─ podman build .
STEP 1: FROM fedora AS builder
STEP 2: RUN mkdir -p /dir1/dir2 && touch /dir1/dir2/file1 /dir1/dir2/file2 /dir1/dir2/file3
--> Using cache e7688a1b901697667da1b6b8eaaedfe8d607f17a5e2784bd28435700ccb6d72c
STEP 3: FROM fedora
STEP 4: RUN mkdir /dir1
--> Using cache c833352cb84fb7d58783327ea7d9b3677c437a86314586c710a9372b9bf4eda7
STEP 5: COPY --from=builder /dir1/dir2/file1 /dir1/
Error: error building at STEP "COPY --from=builder /dir1/dir2/file1 /dir1/": no files found matching "/home/jbauers/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/3decf905202817b53228c65972d14070e165e8a0c7ae7e576d8ecfec2694e8b1/merged/dir1/dir2/file1": no such file or directory
```
```
╰─ podman version
Version:            1.5.1
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.7
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

Thanks!
I confirm: I can reproduce the bug ;-)
Thanks for the reproducer, we'll take another look.  @qiwang17 can you give the new Dockerfile a run through please?
I'll try tagging the right Qi this time.   @QiWang19

in podman 1.5.1 I see the error after I change the line `COPY --from=builder /dir1/dir2/file1 /dir1/` of the Dockerfile. But I can't regenerate this error with master branch podman or buildah.

  podman 1.5.1 build use same Dockerfile twice, cache works

```
// podman 1.5.1
$ podman build .
STEP 1: FROM fedora AS builder
STEP 2: RUN mkdir -p /dir1/dir2 && touch /dir1/dir2/file1 /dir1/dir2/file2 /dir1/dir2/file3
--> Using cache fb3f2e4ac0379f8b929b7b3a141fb019904d3f2e85b8641ada9bca2170077f05
STEP 3: FROM fedora
STEP 4: RUN mkdir /dir1
--> Using cache 8736da636d35ba9b0e12bf69b8b62a8308e7aae9a66b196d3adc8b62e6e887ad
STEP 5: COPY --from=builder /dir1/dir2/file1 /dir1/
--> Using cache 89f6e0c69ec57f50651bff17c1bb9ea973365e075583d45639a1f689a225cd00
```
change the Dockerfile COPY --from=builder /dir1/dir2/file1 to COPY --from=builder /dir1/dir2/file2
```
// podman 1.5.1
$ podman build .
STEP 1: FROM fedora AS builder
STEP 2: RUN mkdir -p /dir1/dir2 && touch /dir1/dir2/file1 /dir1/dir2/file2 /dir1/dir2/file3
--> Using cache 269c22ca168a583e5dc051ab13abdf6f0ab41eed483c9ef5603837d2a6a28b6c
STEP 3: FROM fedora
STEP 4: RUN mkdir /dir1
--> Using cache 6349f9cee535bfd1fe9d88b6e81deba877f2ff3f09f24937db156109e1a13d9d
STEP 5: COPY --from=builder /dir1/dir2/file2 /dir1/
Error: error building at STEP "COPY --from=builder /dir1/dir2/file2 /dir1/": no files found matching "/home/qiwan/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/2748053c79264ecc8aa37319045385fc894a1f31aa8938ae507d0c3c0313e739/merged/dir1/dir2/file2": no such file or directory
```
@TomSweeneyRedHat @QiWang19 Is this fixed? I know we've had a few Buildah revendors since
Lets marked this as fixed.  Reopen if I am mistaken.
@rhatdan, just confirmed that it's fixed in at least upstream, and I believe Podman v1.6.2
